title: cistus × revolii nothosubsp. grandiflorus
intro: Cistus × revolii nothosubsp. grandiflorus, also known as the 'GranDus' or 'Revol's Rock Rose', is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a hybrid species of Cistus ladanifer and Cistus parviflorus, known for its fragrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Cistus × revolii nothosubsp. grandiflorus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help to promote overall health and well-being by reducing stress and improving respiratory function.
